sections.label: Section 112
sections.original: Χαίρειν , Ἀθάνα , τἄλλ ’ ἐγώ σ ’ ἐφίεμαι ,
sections.translation: In all else , Athena , I bid you take your pleasure , but he will pay this penalty and no other .

sections.label: Section 114
sections.original: Σὺ δ ’ οὖν , ἐπειδὴ τέρψις ἥδε σοι τὸ δρᾷν ,
sections.translation: Well , then , since it delights you to do so , put your arm to use ; spare no portion of your plan .

sections.label: Section 116
sections.original: Χωρῶ πρὸς ἔργον · τοῦτό σοι δ ’ ἐφίεμαι ,
sections.translation: I go to my work . And I give you this commission : be always for me the close-standing ally that you have been for me today !

sections.label: Section 118
sections.original: Ὁρᾷς , Ὀδυσσεῦ , τὴν θεῶν ἰσχὺν , ὅση ;
sections.translation: Do you see , Odysseus , how great is the strength of the gods ? Whom could you have found more prudent than this man ,

sections.label: Section 120
sections.original: ἢ δρᾷν ἀμείνων εὑρέθη τὰ καίρια ;
sections.translation: or better able to do what the situation demanded ?

sections.label: Section 121
sections.original: ἐγὼ μὲν οὐδέν ’ οἶδ ’· ἐποικτείρω δέ νιν
sections.translation: I know of no one , but in his misery I pity him all the same , even though he hates me , because he is yoked beneath a ruinous delusion — I think of my own lot no less than his .

sections.label: Section 125
sections.original: ὁρῶ γὰρ ἡμάς οὐδὲν ὄντας ἄλλο πλὴν
sections.translation: For I see that all we who live are nothing more than phantoms or fleeting shadow .

sections.label: Section 127
sections.translation: Therefore since you witness his fate , see that you yourself never utter an arrogant word against the gods , nor assume any swelling pride , if in the scales of fate you are weightier

sections.label: Section 130
sections.original: ἢ χειρὶ βρίθεις ἢ μακροῦ πλούτου βάθει .
sections.translation: than another in strength of hand or in depth of ample wealth . For a day can press down all human things , and a day can raise them up . But the gods embrace men of sense and abhor the evil .

sections.label: Section 134
sections.translation: Son of Telamon , you who hold

sections.label: Section 135
sections.translation: your throne on wave-washed Salamis near the open sea , when your fortune is fair , I rejoice with you . But whenever the stroke of Zeus , or the raging rumor of the Danaans with the clamor of their evil tongues attacks you , then I shrink with great fear and shudder in terror ,

sections.label: Section 140
sections.translation:
like the fluttering eye of the winged dove .
Just so with the passing of the night loud tumults oppressed us to our dishonor , telling how you visited the meadow wild with horses and destroyed

sections.label: Section 145
sections.translation:
the cattle of the Greeks , their spoil , prizes of the spear which had not yet been shared , how you killed them with flashing iron .
Such are the whispered slanders that Odysseus moulds and breathes into the ears of all ,

sections.label: Section 150
sections.original: καὶ σφόδρα πείθει · περὶ γὰρ σοῦ νῦν
sections.translation:
and he wins much belief . For now he tells tales concerning you that easily win belief , and each hearer rejoices with spiteful scorn at your burdens more than he who told .
Point your shaft at a noble spirit ,

sections.label: Section 155
sections.original: οὐκ ἂν ἁμάρτοι · κατὰ δ ’ ἄν τις ἐμοῦ
sections.translation: and you could not miss ; but if a man were to speak such things against me , he would win no belief . It is on the powerful that envy creeps . Yet the small without the great are a teetering tower of defence .

sections.label: Section 160
sections.original: μετὰ γὰρ μεγάλων βαιὸς ἄριστ ’ ἂν ,
sections.translation:
For the lowly stand most upright and prosperous when allied with the great , and the great when served by less .
But foolish men cannot learn good precepts in these matters beforehand . It is men of this sort that subject you to tumult , and

sections.label: Section 165
sections.translation: we lack the power to repel these charges without you , O King . For when they have escaped your eye , they chatter like flocking birds . But , terrified by a mighty vulture ,

sections.label: Section 170
sections.original: τάχ ’ ἂν , ἐξαίφνης εἰ σὺ φανείης ,
sections.translation: perhaps , if you should appear , they would quickly cower without voice in silence .

sections.label: Section 172
sections.original: ᾖ ῥά σε Ταυροπόλα Διὸς Ἄρτεμις ,
sections.translation: Was it Artemis ruler of bulls , Zeus ’ s daughter , that drove you , O powerful Rumor , O mother of my shame ,

sections.label: Section 175
sections.translation: drove you against the herds of all our people ? Was she exacting retribution , perhaps , for a victory that had paid her no tribute , whether it was because she had been cheated of the glory of captured arms , or because a stag had been slain without gifts for recompense ? Or can the bronze-cuirassed Lord of War

sections.label: Section 180
sections.translation: have had some cause for anger arising out of an alliance of spears , and taken vengeance for the outrage by contrivance shrouded in night ?

sections.label: Section 182
sections.translation: For never of your own heart alone , son of Telamon , would you have gone so far down the sinister path

sections.label: Section 185
sections.translation:
as to fall upon the flocks . When the gods send madness , it cannot but reach its target , but may Zeus and Phoebus avert the evil rumor of the Greeks !
And if it is the great kings who slander you with their furtive stories ,

sections.label: Section 190
sections.translation: or if it is he born of the abject line of Sisyphus , do not , my king , do not win me an evil name by keeping your face still hidden in the tent by the sea .

sections.label: Section 193
sections.translation: Come now , up from your seat , wherever you are settled in this long-lived pause from battle

sections.label: Section 195
sections.translation: and are making the flame of disaster blaze up to the sky ! The violent insolence of your enemies rushes fearlessly about in the breezy glens , while the tongues of all the army cackle out a load of grief .

sections.label: Section 200
sections.translation: For me , sorrow stands firmly planted .

sections.label: Section 201
sections.translation: Mates of the ship of Ajax , offspring of the race that springs from the Erechtheids , the soil ’ s sons , cries of grief are the portion of us who care from afar for the house of Telamon .

sections.label: Section 205
sections.translation: Ajax , our terrible , mighty lord of untamed power , now lies plagued by a turbid storm of disease .

sections.label: Section 208
sections.translation: And what is the heavy change from the fortune of yesterday which this night has produced ?

sections.label: Section 210
sections.translation: Daughter of Teleutas the Phrygian , speak , since for you his spear-won mate bold Ajax maintains his love , so that with some knowledge you could suggest an explanation .

sections.label: Section 214
sections.translation: Oh , how am I to tell a tale too terrible for words ?

sections.label: Section 215
sections.translation: Grave as death is the suffering which you will hear . By madness our glorious Ajax was seized in the night , and he has been subjected to utter disgrace . All this you may see inside his dwelling — butchered victims bathed in blood ,

sections.label: Section 220
sections.translation: sacrifices of no hand but his .

sections.label: Section 221
sections.translation: What report of the fiery warrior have you revealed to us , unbearable , nor yet escapable —

sections.label: Section 225
sections.translation: a report which the great Danaans propound , which their powerful storytelling spreads ! Ah , me , I shudder at the future ’ s advancing step . In public view the man will die

sections.label: Section 230
sections.translation: because the dark sword in his frenzied hand massacred the herds and the horse-guiding herdsmen .

sections.label: Section 231
sections.original: ὦ μοι , κεῖθεν , κεῖθεν ἄρ ’ ἡμῖν
sections.translation: Ah ! Then it was from there , from there that he came to me with his captive flock !

sections.label: Section 235
sections.translation: Of part , he cut the throats on the floor inside ; some , striking their sides , he tore asunder . Then he caught up two white-footed rams and sheared off the head of one and its tongue-tip , and flung them away ;

sections.label: Section 240
sections.translation: the other he bound upright to a pillar , and seizing a heavy strap from a horse ’ s harness he flogged it with a whistling , doubled lash , while he cursed it with awful imprecations which a god , and no mortal , had taught him .

sections.label: Section 245
sections.translation: The time has come for each of us to veil his head and steal away on foot , or to sit and take on the swift yoke of rowing ,

sections.label: Section 250
sections.translation: giving her way to the sea-faring ship . So angry are the threats which the brother-kings , the sons of Atreus , speed against us ! I fear to share in bitter death beneath an onslaught of stones ,

sections.label: Section 255
sections.translation: crushed at this man ’ s side , whom an untouchable fate holds in its grasp .

sections.label: Section 257
sections.translation: It grips him no longer . For like a southerly wind after it has started up sharply without bright lightning he grows calm . And now in his right mind he has new pain .

sections.label: Section 260
sections.original:
265
Πότερα δ ’ ἂν , εἰ νέμοι τις αἵρεσιν , λάβοις ,
sections.translation: To look on self-made suffering , when no other has had a hand in it — this induces sharp pains .

sections.label: Section 263
sections.original: Τό τοι διπλάζον , ὦ γύναι , μεῖζον κακόν .
sections.translation: But if he has stopped his madness , I have good hope that all may yet be well , since the trouble is of less account once it has passed .

sections.label: Section 265
sections.original: Πῶς τοῦτ ’ ἔλεξας ; οὐ κάτοιδ ’ ὅπως λέγεις . 270
sections.translation: And which , if the choice were given you , would you choose — to distress your friends , and have joy yourself , or to share the grief of friends who grieve ?

sections.label: Section 268
sections.original: ἡμᾶς δὲ τοὺς φρονοῦντας ἠνία ξυνών ·
sections.translation: The twofold sorrow , lady , is certainly the greater evil .

sections.label: Section 269
sections.original: νῦν δ ’, ὡς ἔληξε κἀνέπνευσε τῆς νόσου ,
sections.translation: Then we are ruined now , although the plague is past .

sections.label: Section 270
sections.original: κεῖνός τε λύπῃ πᾶς ἐλήλαται κακῇ , 275
sections.translation: What do you mean ? I do not understand what you say .

sections.label: Section 271
sections.original: ἡμάς θ ’ ὁμοίως οὐδὲν ἧσσον ἢ πάρος .
sections.translation: That man , while afflicted , found joy for himself in the dire fantasies that held him , though his presence distressed us who were sane . But now , since he has had pause and rest from the plague ,

sections.label: Section 275
sections.original: μηδέν τι μᾶλλον ἢ νοσῶν εὐφραίνεται ; 280
sections.translation: he has been utterly subjected to lowly anguish , and we similarly grieve no less than before . Surely , then , these are two sorrows , instead of one ?

sections.label: Section 278
sections.translation: Indeed , I agree , and so I fear that a blow sent by a god has hit him . How could it be otherwise , if his spirit is no lighter

sections.label: Section 280
sections.original: κεῖνος γὰρ ἄκρας νυκτὸς , ἡνίχ ’ ἕσπεροι 285
sections.translation: than when he was plagued , now that he is released ?

sections.label: Section 281
sections.translation: This , you must know , is how matters stand .

sections.label: Section 282
sections.translation: In what way did the plague first swoop down on him ? Tell us who share your pain how it happened .

sections.label: Section 284
sections.original: Αἴας ; τί τήνδ ’ ἄκλητος οὔθ ’ ὑπ ’ ἀγγέλων
sections.translation: You will hear all that took place , since you are involved .

sections.label: Section 285
sections.original: κληθεὶς ἀφορμᾷς πεῖραν , οὔτε του κλύων 290
sections.translation: In the dead of night when the evening lamps were no longer aflame , he seized a two-edged sword and wanted to leave on an aimless foray . Then I admonished him and said , What are you doing , Ajax ? Why do you set out unsummoned on this expedition ,

sections.label: Section 290
sections.original: καὶ τὰς ὲκεῖ μὲν οὐκ ἔχω λέγειν πάθας . 295
sections.translation: neither called by messenger , nor warned by trumpet ? In fact the whole army is sleeping now . But he answered me curtly with that trite jingle : Woman , silence graces woman . And I , taking his meaning , desisted , but he rushed out alone .

sections.label: Section 295
sections.original:
300
ᾐκίζεθ ’, ὥστε φῶτας , ὲν ποίμναις πίτνων .
sections.translation: What happened out there , I cannot tell . But he came in with his captives hobbled together — bulls , herding dogs , and his fleecy quarry . Some he beheaded ; of some he cut the twisted throat or broke the spine ; others
